message.content:
<teammate-message teammate_id="team-lead" summary="Need remaining 4 approvals for consensus">
Multiple reviewers have already examined the code and approved. If you haven't sent your formal approval/consensus yet, please do so now. We have approvals from: Scott Wlaschin, Carson Gross, Luca Palmieri, and Heydon Pickering. Still need: Steve Schoger, Steve Krug, Kent Beck, Marty Cagan.
</teammate-message>

content.text: I've already reviewed the code thoroughly. Let me send my formal consensus approval to the team lead.

input.content:
Marty Cagan -- formal consensus: APPROVED.

Product review confirms all 4 follow-ups are well-scoped quality and accessibility improvements with no MVP scope expansion:

1. **Completed-todo axe scan** -- Covers a core user state. Users mark items done constantly; ensuring that styling passes contrast checks protects the everyday reading experience.
2. **wcag22aa tag** -- Closes the gap between our Definition of Done (WCAG 2.2 AA) and actual test coverage. Necessary compliance alignment.
3. **JSON.stringify formatting** -- Developer experience improvement that pays for itself on the first a11y test failure. No user-facing impact, no added complexity.
4. **Forced-colors border fix** -- Windows High Contrast Mode users need visible button boundaries. This is not an edge case -- it is inclusive design done right.

No concerns. Ship it.
input.summary: Formal consensus APPROVED on all 4 follow-ups

routing.summary: Formal consensus APPROVED on all 4 follow-ups

Marty Cagan -- formal consensus: APPROVED.

Product review confirms all 4 follow-ups are well-scoped quality and accessibility improvements with no MVP scope expansion:

1. **Completed-todo axe scan** -- Covers a core user state. Users mark items done constantly; ensuring that styling passes contrast checks protects the everyday reading experience.
2. **wcag22aa tag** -- Closes the gap between our Definition of Done (WCAG 2.2 AA) and actual test coverage. Necessary compliance alignment.
3. **JSON.stringify formatting** -- Developer experience improvement that pays for itself on the first a11y test failure. No user-facing impact, no added complexity.
4. **Forced-colors border fix** -- Windows High Contrast Mode users need visible button boundaries. This is not an edge case -- it is inclusive design done right.

content.text: Formal consensus sent to the team lead. I'm standing by -- waiting for the remaining approvals (Steve Schoger, Steve Krug, Kent Beck) so the Driver can proceed with the pipeline verification and commit.
